text.title: AGENTS.md Context Pyramid Core Rules
text.summary: 8 testable rules with rationale + source for kb-agents-md-context-pyramid. Includes terminal skip-this-methodology rule.

rule.statement: Root `AGENTS.md` MUST be ≤80 lines and contain: project identity, key paths table, command quick-reference, gotchas. Detail goes to `.agents/`.
rule.rationale: Anthropic Claude Code docs — root files auto-load on every task; brevity preserves budget.

rule.statement: Every directory containing source code MUST have its own `AGENTS.md` (≤80 lines): what this dir IS, file table, key types.
rule.rationale: Without per-dir files, agents reconstruct context from scratch every time.

rule.statement: Each `CLAUDE.md` MUST contain only `@AGENTS.md` — Claude Code reads `CLAUDE.md` automatically; AGENTS.md is the cross-tool canonical.
rule.rationale: Codex, Cursor, and other tools read AGENTS.md; pointing CLAUDE.md to it unifies the convention.

rule.statement: Deep reference docs MUST live in `.agents/<topic>.md`; loaded only when the agent calls Read.
SDD lifecycle docs (specs, designs, test plans, implementation plans) MUST live in `.aidocs/`; never collapsed into AGENTS.md.
rule.rationale: Project lifecycle ≠ project context; separation prevents bloat.

rule.statement: AGENTS.md links MUST be relative to the AGENTS.md location, NOT to repo root (so a moved dir still navigates).
Audit script MUST run pre-commit: every dir with code has AGENTS.md, AGENTS.md ≤80 lines, no TBDs.
rule.rationale: Without audit the convention rots inside a quarter.

rule.statement: If single-directory project or team uses a different convention, return `skipped`.
